title: ‘A day of hope, peace and unity’: Donald & Melania Trump extend Holi greetings
description: US President Donald Trump extended warm greetings for Holi, recognizing the Hindu festival as a harbinger of spring and a symbol of good triumphing over evil. He conveyed wishes for hope, peace, and unity to all celebrating this vibrant festival of colours. The message underscores Holi's significance in fostering renewal and strengthening community bonds, particularly for the Indian diaspora.

Despite reaching the last four, India's catching efficiency is the lowest in the tournament. Patel praised Sanju Samson's match-winning knock and Jasprit Bumrah's crucial wickets, emphasizing the need for early breakthroughs against England's strong batting lineup.

title: Tariff may be hiked to 15% this week: Scott Bessent
description: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent indicated President Trump's plan to raise universal tariffs to 15% could happen this week. While a temporary 10% levy is in place, authorities aim to reinstate previous tariffs within five months. Bessent also downplayed oil market risks from Middle East tensions, citing ample global supply and US support measures.

Middle East tensions: Oil companies may cushion impact of spike - report
description: Oil marketing companies are absorbing recent crude oil price spikes, creating a buffer against inflation and GDP impacts. Despite a 16.8% rise in Brent crude, a full pass-through to consumers is unlikely, with OMCs expected to manage the increase through their margins. This strategy helps cushion India's economy from geopolitical shocks affecting its heavy reliance on imported oil.

Despite facing constitutional crises and internal party dissent, veteran politician KP Sharma Oli, a former dissident who spent years in prison, remains a formidable force. He is now seeking re-election from his long-held constituency, facing a direct challenge from a younger contender.

title: Oxford museum set to return 500-year-old bronze statue taken from Tamil Nadu temple
description: A 16th-century bronze statue of Saint Tirumankai Alvar, taken from a Tamil Nadu temple, is returning to India from the UK. Acquired by the Ashmolean Museum in 1967, its provenance became questionable after a 2019 discovery. Following an investigation and a formal claim by India, the museum has agreed to its repatriation, highlighting a commitment to ethical collections.

Similar directives were issued for Saudi Arabia, Oman, and Cyprus, with American citizens advised to reconsider travel to these nations due to rising safety risks and disrupted flights.

title: Maharashtra transporters strike: Trucks, buses, taxis to go off roads from Friday
description: Transporters across Maharashtra have announced an indefinite strike starting Thursday midnight, protesting the alleged misuse of the e-challan system and arbitrary penalties. Talks with the transport commissioner failed, leading to the escalation of the protest, which has garnered support from private car owners and school bus operators. The strike aims to address issues like harassment by officers and unjustified fines.

title: Oxford’s Ashmolean Museum returns 16th century bronze sculpture of Thirumangai Alvar
description: The Ashmolean acquired the statue in good faith in 1967. According to the Sotheby’s catalogue, the bronze was sold by the private collector, J.R. Belmont (1886–1981).  There is no information on how the bronze entered his collection.

Indian carriers planned 58 flights on Wednesday, with IndiGo and Air India increasing capacity on long-haul routes. Airports in Dubai and Abu Dhabi are handling limited repatriation flights, while services in Bahrain, Doha, and Kuwait remain suspended.

Mark Zuckerberg is done with Meta's highest-paid employee, this reorganisation proves
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg has quietly dismantled Alexandr Wang's power nine months after the $14 billion hire. The 28-year-old data labeling entrepreneur now oversees AI models being built on infrastructure he doesn't control. A new applied AI engineering organization under Andrew Bosworth routes talent and data pipelines around Wang entirely. Researchers report to other executives. Wang keeps his title but loses every lever that matters—a calculated pivot that suggests Zuckerberg's bet has already failed.

title: OpenAI loses 1.5mn subscribers in less than 48 hours after Altman backs deal that Anthropic rejected
description: OpenAI faces a significant user exodus, with over 1.5 million reportedly leaving ChatGPT after agreeing to let the Pentagon use its AI on classified networks. This backlash, fueled by previous contracts and donations, has seen rival Anthropic's Claude chatbot surge in popularity. Users are now seeking ways to export their ChatGPT data and migrate to Claude.

title: OpenAI is changing contract with Pentagon; Altman says: I would rather go to jail…
description: OpenAI has amended its Pentagon deal following public backlash over AI surveillance concerns. CEO Sam Altman stated he would refuse unconstitutional orders, and the updated contract now explicitly prohibits intentional domestic surveillance of US persons. The Pentagon also confirmed OpenAI services won't be used by intelligence agencies like the NSA without further contract changes.
